title: Parallel Agent Review Improves Evidence Recall In Literature Tasks
author: A. Rivera and S. Chen
summary: A controlled evaluation found that assigning separate search framings to parallel agents increased source diversity and evidence recall compared with a single deterministic agent.
claims: Parallel search agents increase evidence recall when each agent explores a distinct framing.
Fan-in synthesis improves report coherence when claims are deduplicated before ranking.
Uncriticized parallel outputs can increase unsupported claims because weak abstracts are repeated.

title: Reviewer Agents Reduce Unsupported Claims In Automated Research
author: M. Okafor
summary: Benchmark runs showed that a critic pass after extraction reduced unsupported claims, but increased runtime and sometimes over-penalized novel hypotheses.
claims: Critic agents reduce unsupported claims by challenging evidence gaps after extraction.
A critic pass increases runtime and may decrease novelty if applied too early.
Contradiction checks are most useful before final synthesis.

title: Entropy And Framing Diversity In Open-Ended Research Agents
author: L. Singh
summary: The paper argues that open-ended research benefits from early framing diversity and later convergence through critique and synthesis.
claims: Open-ended research benefits from broad early exploration before constraints are tightened.
Entropy injection can reveal overlooked mechanisms and assumptions.
Too much exploration decreases precision when no critic-driven follow-up is used.

title: Structured Artifact Stores For Reproducible Agent Research
author: N. Patel
summary: This systems note describes JSONL traces and typed artifact tables as a practical foundation for reproducible multi-agent runs.
claims: Typed artifact stores make multi-agent research runs easier to audit.
JSONL trace logs are sufficient for early reproducibility when prompts, tools, runtime, and outputs are captured.
Persistent world models should be introduced after the artifact schema stabilizes.

title: Single-Agent Baselines Remain Competitive For Narrow Research Questions
author: D. Morales
summary: A comparison found that single deterministic agents can match multi-agent systems on narrow questions with clear evaluation criteria.
claims: Single deterministic agents can match multi-agent systems on narrow bounded research questions.
Multi-agent fan-out has less advantage when the task already has clear success criteria.
Parallel research increases cost and can decrease precision if agents duplicate similar searches.

title: Risk Controls For Adaptive Agent Harnesses
author: E. Novak
summary: Adaptive harness changes should be proposed as constrained config variants and evaluated before being accepted.
claims: Harness changes should be proposed, evaluated, and gated before modifying execution behavior.
Adaptive evolution without benchmark comparison can worsen reliability.
Pending change schemas improve reviewability and prevent arbitrary self-modification.

title: Orderbook Prediction Market Challenge
author: Dan Robinson
summary: A binary prediction-market optimization challenge where passive market makers quote a YES contract against retail flow, a static competitor ladder, and an informed arbitrageur that sweeps stale quotes.
claims: Positive edge comes from capturing spread against uninformed retail order flow.
Negative edge comes from stale quotes that are swept by an informed arbitrageur after fair-value moves.
The static competitor does not re-anchor to fair value after jumps, creating an opportunity for adaptive strategies.

title: Prediction Market Strategy Design Notes
author: research-harness
summary: Local challenge notes for optimizing passive prediction-market market-making strategies using adaptive fair-value estimation, inventory skew, cancel logic, and quote sizing.
claims: Adaptive fair-value estimates should use competitor midpoint, recent fills, and stale-fill signals.
Canceling or widening quotes after jump or adverse-selection signals reduces arbitrageur losses.
Inventory skew and moderate size help preserve profitability across randomized regimes.

title: Prediction Market Local Evaluator Rubric
author: research-harness
summary: The local evaluator maps generated strategy text to a parameterized passive market maker and scores mean edge across deterministic randomized regimes.
claims: The evaluator rewards adaptive estimation, inventory control, stale-quote protection, and passive liquidity provision.
The evaluator penalizes static ladders, excessive size, and tight quotes without jump guards.
The registered evaluator name is prediction_market and can be used with optimize_query mode.
